body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-14T07:25:22</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>1</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>45</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>14/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>26</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>95</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>On Standby</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>0.5</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>-0.3</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>1</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate/Strong 41-60 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>NW</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>Light</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Fair</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Cloudy</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Cloudy. Very high chance of snow showers, becoming less likely this evening. Winds west to northwesterly 35 to 45 km/h tending west to southwesterly in the middle of the day then tending south to southwesterly 30 to 40 km/h in the late evening.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Rugged mountain weather and more fun turns</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It's another morning of soft and forgiving turns for the resilient.&nbsp; We've had another 1 cm of wet snow overnight, and there is more borderline precipitation coming down as I write.&nbsp; The current temp is just above zero (heading for a top of 1 degrees), the wind has backed off a notch but is still a stiff northwesterly, making the wind-chill about minus 10 up the top.</p><p>Don't let a little rugged mountain weather put you off, as the turns will be fun out there today.&nbsp; The wind may have dropped enough to open another lift or two above the road, but be sure to don your good gear and check the <a href="https://www.mthotham.com.au/mountain/conditions/lifts-terrain" rel="nofollow">lift status</a> before heading out there.&nbsp; And if storm riding isn't your thing, it looks like we'll have plenty of great weather days once things clear up overnight.&nbsp;</p><p>Village is scheduled to open at 7.30am this morning.&nbsp; Enjoy the ride!</p><p>Cheers Bill</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
    <LiftsOpen>0</LiftsOpen>
    <LiftsStandby>0</LiftsStandby>
    <LiftsClosed>0</LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
    <TerrainParks>2</TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>It's a bad day for the backcountry.  The visibility is poor, the weather is wet and the cover is thin and punchy.  Leave it for another day but take care if you must head out there. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
